characters.name: Arthur Adams
characters.description: Arthur AdamsBiographical InformationAlias(es)Agent AdamsOccupationCapoStatusAliveRelationshipsAffiliationLodge Family(formerly)RelativesUnnamed fatherUnnamed dogAppearancesActorJohn BehlmannFirst Appearance"Chapter Twenty-Three: The Blackboard Jungle"Last Appearance"Chapter Thirty: The Noose Tightens"[Source] Unnamed dog Arthur Adamsis a recurring character onThe CW'sRiverdale. He is portrayed byJohn Behlmann. Adams was initially presented as a reasonably serious agent, who was dedicated to doing his job and being thorough in his investigation intoHiram Lodge's criminal activities. However, it was later revealed that he isn't an agent at all, but rather a fairly manipulative capo forHermione Lodge.

characters.name: Elaina Alexis
characters.description: Elaina AlexisBiographical InformationStatusDeceasedAppearancesActorMädchen AmickFirst Appearance"Chapter Ninety-Nine: The Witching Hour(s)"Last Appearance"Chapter Ninety-Nine: The Witching Hour(s)"[Source] Elaina Alexisis a character onThe CW'sRiverdale. She is portrayed byMädchen Amick. Elaina was one ofPoppy's friends, who frequented her salons.

characters.name: Vittorio Alto
characters.description: Vittorio AltoBiographical InformationOccupationGangsterOwner of Cucina Sacasa restaurantStatusDeceasedCause of DeathShot in the head(byHiram Lodge)AppearancesActorLouis FerreiraFirst Appearance"Chapter Eighty-Eight: Citizen Lodge"Last Appearance"Chapter Eighty-Eight: Citizen Lodge"[Source] Owner of Cucina Sacasa restaurant Vittorio "Vito" Altowas a guest starring character onThe CW'sRiverdale. He was portrayed byLouis Ferreira. The stereotypical Italian mobster, Vito Alto was a ruthless gangster who hadRiverdale's criminal underground in his hands. Vito used his charm and wealth to attract the attention of young people like Jaime that he could use as a lackey. Vito was arrogant and dangerous, and he inspired fear rather than respect in people. Old and sick, Vito lost all his power and had no chance against his former dealer, who grow up to become the infamousHiram Lodge.

characters.name: Amalia
characters.description: AmaliaBiographical InformationSpeciesWerewolfStatusDeceasedCause of DeathStabbed in the back(bySabrina)RelationshipsRelativesNicholas Scratch(master)AppearancesFirst Appearance"Chapter Fourteen: Lupercalia"Last Appearance"Chapter Fourteen: Lupercalia"[Source] Amaliawas awerewolfandNicholas Scratch'familiarbefore she was killed bySabrina. She was gifted to Nick at birth, and when his family, died, she was all he had left. However, she became increasingly jealous after Nick began attending theAcademy of Unseen Artsand started dating. So, he banished her to theMountains of Madness. Amalia tracksNickdown toBaxter High's library, where he andSabrinanarrowly escape out the main doors before he seals the doors shut and banishes Amalia.

characters.name: Amanda
characters.description: AmandaBiographical InformationOccupationCo-interim department head(formerly)Personal ShopperStatusAliveRelationshipsAffiliationLacy's Department StoreAppearancesActorHeléne YorkeFirst Appearance"Chapter One: Once Upon a Time in New York"Last Appearance"Chapter Thirteen: Come Together"[Source] Personal Shopper  Amanda is a very entitled, snooty woman, believing she deserved a promotion all because she was born and raised in the Upper East Side of New York City. Amanda is ambitious and manipulative. She is gossiping and can be mean to achieve her goals, without necessarily realizing how bad her words might hurt. Although Amanda fought Katy for the job as assistant to iconic Gloria Grandbilt, Amanda's true loyalty is to herself.

characters.name: Andre
characters.description: AndreBiographical InformationOccupationPersonal AssistantCapoStatusDeceasedCause of DeathHead trauma(bySmall Fry Boucher)RelationshipsAffiliationLodge FamilyAppearancesActorStephan MiersFirst Appearance"Chapter Fifteen: Nighthawks"Last Appearance"Chapter Thirty-Four: Judgment Night"[Source] Capo Andrewas a recurring character onThe CW'sRiverdale. He was portrayed byStephan Miers. Andre was initially presented asSmithers' replacement. Although, in light of recent events, he has proven to be much more. More than just a doorman and chauffeur, Andre was theLodges' capo, tasked with doing their dirty work and protectingVeronicaat all cost.

characters.name: Archie Andrews (Rivervale)
characters.description: Archie AndrewsBiographical InformationBornJuly 1, 1995(age: 25)OccupationRROTC InstructorStatusAlive(resurrected)Cause of DeathStabbed/heart removed(byCheryl; 1st time)Shot in the back of the head(byBetty; 2nd time)RelationshipsAffiliationRivervale High SchoolRelativesAsher Andrews(ancestor)Fred Andrews †(father)Mary Andrews(mother)Frank Andrews(uncle)Unborn son †AppearancesActorKJ ApaFirst Appearance"Chapter Ninety-Six: Welcome to Rivervale"Last Appearance"Chapter One Hundred and Sixteen: The Stand"[Source] Shot in the back of the head(byBetty; 2nd time) Fred Andrews †(father)Mary Andrews(mother)Frank Andrews(uncle)Unborn son † Archie shares a lot of similar personality traits with hisRiverdalecounterpart. He's kind-hearted, compassionate, and loyal to his community, demonstrated by his attempt to helpRivervale's depleting maple supply. However, unlike his Riverdale counterpart, this version of Archie is a cold-blooded killer, killing at least three of his friends without any remorse.

characters.name: Artie Andrews
characters.description: Artie Andrews1944PresentBiographical InformationOccupationWar VeteranFire Department ChiefStatusDeceasedRelationshipsAffiliationU.S. MilitaryRiverdale Fire DepartmentRelativesArchibald Andrews †(paternal ancestor)Virginia Andrews(wife)Oscar Andrews †(son)Fred Andrews†(son)Frank Andrews†(son)Mary Andrews†(ex-daughter-in-law)Archie Andrews†(grandson)AppearancesActorKJ ApaPaul JarrettFirst Appearance"Chapter Fifty-Eight: In Memoriam"Last Appearance"Chapter One Hundred and Six: Angels in America"[Source] Virginia Andrews(wife)Oscar Andrews †(son)Fred Andrews†(son)Frank Andrews†(son)Mary Andrews†(ex-daughter-in-law)Archie Andrews†(grandson) Arthur "Artie" Andrewsis a character onThe CW'sRiverdale. He is portrayed by Paul Jarret in the present and byKJ Apaduring flashbacks. Artie, like his sonsFredandFrankand his grandsonArchie, was a man of unwavering courage and principle. As a veteran and former chief of theRiverdale Fire Department, he exemplified dedication and loyalty to his country and community. His commitment to serving others extended beyond his role as a firefighter; when faced with injustice, such as the attempt to turnRiverdaleinto a sundown town in 1944, Artie took a stand, advocating for equality and freedom. His compassionate nature was evident in his willingness to assist persecuted families and his empathy towards those in need. Artie's influence didn't end with his passing; his presence lingered in the memories and dreams of his loved ones, inspiring Archie to revive the Riverdale Fire Department in the face of adversity.

characters.name: Frank Andrews
characters.description: Frank AndrewsBiographical InformationOccupationFootball Player(formerly)SoldierMinerFirefighterCouncilman(formerly)Construction WorkerForemanBasketball coach(1955)StatusDeceasedCause of DeathDecapitation(killed byPercival Pickens; orginal timeline; resurrected)Unknown(killed byChic; 1955 timeline)RelationshipsAffiliationUnited States ArmyRiverdale Fire DepartmentRelativesArchibald Andrews †(paternal ancestor)Artie Andrews†(father)Virginia Andrews(mother)Oscar Andrews †(brother)Fred Andrews†(brother)Mary Andrews(sister-in-law)Archie Andrews(nephew)AppearancesActorRyan RobbinsFirst Appearance"Chapter Sixty-Six: Tangerine"Last Appearance"Chapter One Hundred and Thirty-Seven: Goodbye, Riverdale"[Source] SoldierMinerFirefighterCouncilman(formerly)Construction WorkerForemanBasketball coach(1955) Unknown(killed byChic; 1955 timeline) Frank Andrews is a blue collar, former high school football star – tough, brash, and charming. His temper and vices constantly get him into trouble. He’s lived in the shadow of Fred (the late Luke Perry), his beloved older brother, since he was born. As a result, Frank joined the Army in search of his own identity. After three tours of duty, Frank Andrews is haunted and in need of a home.[1]

characters.name: Mary Andrews
characters.description: Mary AndrewsBiographical InformationOccupationLawyerSaleswoman(1955)Dress-shop Owner(1955)StatusDeceasedCause of DeathUnknownRelationshipsAffiliationPerky In Peach(1955)RelativesFred Andrews†(husband)Archie Andrews†(son)Oscar Andrews †(brother-in-law)Frank Andrews†(brother-in-law)Artie Andrews †(father-in-law)Virginia Andrews(mother-in-law)Brooke Rivers†(ex-wife)AppearancesActorMolly RingwaldFirst Appearance"Chapter Ten: The Lost Weekend"Last Appearance"Chapter One Hundred and Thirty-Seven: Goodbye, Riverdale"[Source] Saleswoman(1955)Dress-shop Owner(1955) Archie Andrews†(son)Oscar Andrews †(brother-in-law)Frank Andrews†(brother-in-law)Artie Andrews †(father-in-law)Virginia Andrews(mother-in-law)Brooke Rivers†(ex-wife) Having left Riverdale two years ago to follow her dreams, Mary returns to town in her family's hour of need. She not only reconnects with Archie and her husband, Fred, but also her former high school gal-pals Hermione and Alice, just in time for a homecoming dance.[2]

characters.name: Ms. Appleyard
characters.description: Ms. AppleyardBiographical InformationOccupationCheerleading coachStatusAliveRelationshipsAffiliationRiverdale High School(formerly)AppearancesActorEmily TennantFirst Appearance"Chapter Sixty-Seven: Varsity Blues"Last Appearance"Chapter Sixty-Seven: Varsity Blues"[Source] Ms. Appleyardis a guest starring character onThe CW'sRiverdale. She is portrayed byEmily Tennant. Appleyard was the new cheerleading coach atRiverdale High School, put in place afterCherylwas deemed unfit byMr. HoneyandMrs. Burble. However, she quit after suffering a panic attack.

Like most demons, Asmodeus is cruel by nature and is willing to kill without remorse of empathy, especially towards mortals.

characters.description: George AugustineBiographical InformationStatusAliveRelationshipsRelativesJeffery Augustine(son)AppearancesActorBlake StadelFirst Appearance"Chapter Fifty-Eight: In Memoriam"Last Appearance"Chapter Seventy-Eight: The Preppy Murders"[Source] George Augustineis a guest starring character onThe CW'sRiverdale. He is portrayed byBlake Stadel. George is charged with the deadly hit-and-run ofFred Andrews, though his teenage son,Jeffery, was the actual driver responsible for the collision.

characters.description: Jeffery AugustineBiographical InformationStatusAliveRelationshipsRelativesGeorge Augustine(father)AppearancesActorRyan GranthamFirst Appearance"Chapter Fifty-Eight: In Memoriam"Last Appearance"Chapter Fifty-Eight: In Memoriam"[Source] Jeffery Augustineis a guest starring character onThe CW'sRiverdale. He is portrayed byRyan Grantham. Jeffery is responsible for the death ofFred Andrews, having hit him with hisfather's truck while Fred was on the side of the road lending a hand to astranded woman.

characters.name: Auteur
characters.description: AuteurBiographical InformationStatusAliveRelationshipsAffiliationBlue Velvet VideoAppearancesActorTrinity LikinsFirst Appearance"Chapter Seventy-Four: Wicked Little Town"Last Appearance"Chapter Seventy-Eight: The Preppy Murders"[Source] TheAuteur, formerly known as theVoyeur, was an anonymous character(s) and one of the main antagonists of thefourthandfifth seasonofThe CW'sRiverdale. Initially believed to be a single individual videotaping localRiverdaleresidences, the Voyeur has evolved into an Auteur, now re-enacting the gruesome murders of deceased residents while wearing masks crafted in their likeness, such as withJason BlossomandJughead Jones. In "Chapter Seventy-Eight: The Preppy Murders", Betty and Jughead discover thatJellybeanis the Auteur and that she became the Auteur not with malice intent but to convince Jughead to stay in Riverdale. With him leaving forStonewall Prepand college soon after that, she created the mystery of the Voyeur in hopes that the investigation would pressure him into staying. With help fromRicky DeSantosand kids from thecommunity center, Jellybean went on to craft masks and props to further her goal.

characters.name: Batibat
characters.description: BatibatBiographical InformationBornUnknown(age: 70+)[1]Alias(es)Hellspawn(byAmbrose)SpeciesSleep DemonStatusAlive(trapped in a jar)RelationshipsAffiliationLilith(creator)AppearancesActorMegan LeitchFirst Appearance"Chapter Four: Witch Academy"Last Appearance"Chapter Twenty-Eight: Sabrina Is Legend"[Source]  Batibatis a recurring character onNetflix'sChilling Adventures of Sabrina. She is portrayed byMegan Leitch. Batibat has proven to be rather vengeful, specifically towardsEdward Spellmanfor imprisoning her. She took her anger out on the Spellman family since she couldn't take it out on Edward. However, she will honor deals; in the original timeline, she was released on the condition that she'd no longer target the Spellmans.

characters.name: Britta Beach
characters.description: Britta BeachBiographical InformationOccupationStudentFootball PlayerBabysitterStatusAliveRelationshipsAffiliationRiverdale High SchoolRiverdale BulldogsRelativesUnnamed motherUnnamed fatherAppearancesActorKyra LerouxFirst Appearance"Chapter Eighty-Two: Back To School"Last Appearance"Chapter One Hundred and Seventeen: Night of the Comet"[Source] Football PlayerBabysitter Brittania "Britta" Beachis a recurring character onThe CW'sRiverdale. She is portrayed byKyra Leroux. Britta Beach is a brave, principled, and fiercely loyal young woman growing up in the often chaotic and surreal world ofRiverdale. First introduced as a student atRiverdale Highwith a passion for football, Britta stands out immediately for her willingness to challenge gender norms and play on the all-boysBulldogs team, demonstrating confidence, determination, and a refusal to be underestimated. Britta’s strong moral compass and quick sense of justice come into sharper focus when she stands up to homophobic bullying, courageously coming out as gay despite not yet being accepted at home. When her parents reject her identity, Britta’s resilience shines through, even as she experiences homelessness, trauma, and isolation. Her vulnerability is met with strength; rather than crumble, she finds a support system inToni TopazandCheryl Blossomand becomes part of their extended chosen family. In Cheryl’s world, Britta shows an uncanny capacity for loyalty and bravery, often serving as Cheryl’s confidante and, later, her literal savior during a supernatural crisis. Despite the threats posed byAbigail Blossom’s possession and magical machinations, Britta never backs down, even infiltrating dreamscapes and risking her own safety to bring Cheryl back. Her level-headedness and clever problem-solving skills make her an unexpected but formidable player in Riverdale’s darker, more arcane storylines. Emotionally intelligent and grounded, Britta is also perceptive—she picks up on the strange shifts in Cheryl’s behavior, empathizes with Toni’s experiences, and shows compassion for others even while coping with her own struggles. She has a strong sense of duty, a subtle sense of humor, and a sharp, often underappreciated intelligence. Ultimately, Britta Beach embodies the strength of someone who has had to grow up too quickly, yet retains a quiet hope for belonging, acceptance, and love. Her journey is one of courage in the face of adversity, and her place in Riverdale is defined by her heart, her integrity, and her unwavering fight for what’s right—even when the odds are terrifyingly against her.

characters.name: Britta Beach (Rivervale)
characters.description: Britta BeachBiographical InformationOccupationStudentStatusAliveAppearancesActorKyra LerouxFirst Appearance"Chapter Ninety-Six: Welcome to Rivervale"Last Appearance"Chapter One Hundred and Fifteen: Return to Rivervale"[Source] Brittania "Britta" Beachis one of the girls atCheryl's boarding school. Britta followsCherylinto the woods, where they confrontArchie,Betty, andKevinfor trespassing onThornhillterritory, where they've come across a sacrificed deer on an altar covered in blood ruins. She warns them to leave unless they want to be shot with arrows by her pupils, led by Britta. Betty informs Cheryl that they're standing in the middle of a crime scene and that she needs to collect all the evidence, including the deer, but Cheryl objects and commands her girls to draw their bows.
